description: 15 duplicate customer records identified by email address. Same email appears with different customer_id values, causing revenue attribution to be split across records.
fix_sql: DELETE FROM customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT MAX(customer_id) FROM customers GROUP BY email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)
impact: Order totals may be inflated due to split customer records. Customer lifetime value calculations are inaccurate for affected accounts.

description: 30 orders have NULL customer_id, making it impossible to attribute these orders to any customer.
fix_sql: SELECT order_id, order_date, total FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NULL ORDER BY order_date
impact: Revenue from these 30 orders cannot be attributed to customer segments, territories, or lifetime value calculations. Estimated unattributed revenue: ~$45,000.

description: 20 orders have dates in non-standard formats (MM/DD/YYYY or DD-Mon-YYYY instead of YYYY-MM-DD). These will be excluded from date-based queries and trend analysis.
fix_sql: UPDATE orders SET order_date = strftime('%Y-%m-%d', order_date) WHERE order_date NOT GLOB '[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]-[0-9][0-9]-[0-9][0-9]'
impact: Time-series analysis and monthly reporting will exclude these 20 orders, potentially understating revenue in affected periods.

description: 10 products have negative unit prices, indicating data entry errors.
fix_sql: UPDATE products SET unit_price = ABS(unit_price) WHERE unit_price < 0
impact: Orders for these products will have incorrect totals. Revenue calculations involving these products are unreliable.

description: 5 orders where the total field does not match quantity * unit_price * (1 - discount). Differences range from 50% to 200% of expected values.
fix_sql: UPDATE orders SET total = ROUND(quantity * (SELECT unit_price FROM products WHERE products.product_id = orders.product_id) * (1 - discount), 2) WHERE order_id IN (SELECT o.order_id FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id WHERE ABS(o.total - ROUND(o.quantity * p.unit_price * (1 - o.discount), 2)) > 0.01)
impact: Revenue reports are overstated by the excess amounts in these 5 orders.

description: 8 return records have a return_date that precedes the corresponding order_date, which is logically impossible.
fix_sql: SELECT r.return_id, r.return_date, o.order_date FROM returns r JOIN orders o ON r.order_id = o.order_id WHERE r.return_date < o.order_date
impact: Return processing timeline analysis is unreliable. These records may indicate data entry errors or system clock issues.
